sutta: [Music] buddha said seeing seeing 5 things monks parents desire a son born in the family what 5 he that is helped he will help us secondly for us he will do what must be done thirdly long will he keep up traditions fourth worthily possess his heritage and fifth make offerings to the ghosts when we are dead monk has seen these 5 things parents desire a son this suta mentions a sunnah but it is equally applicable to a daughter also the first 1 he said that he that is helped he will help us this probably concerns for example think now when they bring up the sun that the sun can also help them for example work in the fields or help them to earn their living secondly for us he will do what must be done that means when parents are old properly they expect the children to take care of them thirdly we will keep up the traditions these are certain family traditions or religious traditions etc that certain [Music] families or certain races or certain people they practice for example chinese they like to have this family ancestral tablets and they they expect the children to to pay respect to this ancestral what they call ancestral worship and some families they have some business and they expect the children to keep up their business and some people they perhaps they are supporters of certain um [Music] ascetics or certain monks and probably they expect the children also to support these monks because that is their religion so this is keeping up the traditions then verbally possess his heritage that means he inherit his parents properties etc he should be worthy of it and lastly make offerings to the ghost these make offerings to the ghost because in the buddhist belief that when a person has become a ghost that person is in a state of uh suffering so going here and there trying to look for food now it might be difficult so sometimes it's quite often that ghosts come back to their relatives and hoping that the relatives could help them so that is why it is an indian tradition that when [Music] our relatives have passed away we make offerings and hoping that the relatives can get it now either we can make offerings and before we make offerings we can pray and tell the the relatives that we are going to make some offering to a temple or something to a monastery and we are doing it on their behalf so that whatever merit or blessing that accrues we will pass it to them there's 1 way another way is in the old indian tradition we read in the sutas is that they make offerings of food and they pray and after that they burn the food that used to be an old brahmin tradition so this is uh some of the things that parents expect their children to do and actually it is good for parents not to tell their children all these things because nowadays some a lot of these things we don't teach the younger generation and they don't study the suttas so they don't know all these things so we have to teach the younger generation all these things because you can't expect them to carry out all these things that you want them to do if they they don't know about them so there are many things that children should be taught and it is a parent's duties to teach them concerning especially concerning religion and the good the duties that they should do that is
